details: Tier names (Trivial, Low, Moderate, High, Critical) consistent across workflow and matrix
All 4 dimensions (Functional, Technical, Team/Org, NFR) present in both files
No full scoring rubric duplication in technical-assessment-guide.md
No conflicting scale definitions (all use unified 0-60)
technical-assessment-guide.md exists and references unified files
Merged file contains process content from epic-decomposition-workflow
Merged file contains domain patterns from feature-decomposition-patterns
Legacy 0-10 scale mapping documented in matrix
Legacy 0-60 tier names (Simple, Moderate, Complex, Enterprise) referenced

key_validations.details: Score ranges 0-10, 11-20, 21-35, 36-50, 51-60 cover full 0-60 with no gaps or overlaps
All dimension names present in both workflow and matrix files
feature-decomposition.md contains both process steps and domain patterns
No broken cross-file references; all cited files exist

observations.note: Cross-file reference integrity is complete. complexity-assessment-workflow.md correctly references complexity-assessment-matrix.md by filename, and technical-assessment-guide.md references both unified files.

Tier naming and ranges are perfectly consistent. All 5 tiers (Trivial 0-10, Low 11-20, Moderate 21-35, High 36-50, Critical 51-60) appear identically in both workflow and matrix files with matching numeric boundaries.

All 4 dimensions from ideation scale are preserved: Functional Complexity, Technical Complexity, Team/Organizational Complexity, and Non-Functional Requirements (NFR). Each dimension appears in workflow calculation steps and matrix scoring rubrics.
No duplicate full scoring rubric exists in technical-assessment-guide.md. File correctly references unified files instead of duplicating content, following AC#2 consolidation requirement.
Redundant source files successfully removed: epic-decomposition-workflow.md and feature-decomposition-patterns.md no longer exist in architecture references. AC#5 cleanup requirement satisfied.

Merged feature-decomposition.md contains both process sections (epic identification, feature grouping) and domain patterns (e-commerce, SaaS). AC#3 merge requirement fully satisfied with content preservation.
Legacy scale mapping documented: complexity-assessment-matrix.md contains both 0-10 scale mapping and 0-60 tier name mapping for backward compatibility. AC#4 requirement met.

recommendations.recommendation: Consider adding a validation script to verify no new scoring scale definitions are accidentally created. STORY-434 unification can be preserved by automated checks in CI/CD.
recommendations.rationale: Future refactorings might reintroduce duplicate scales. Automated validation would catch this early.

recommendations.recommendation: Add a brief note in architecture SKILL.md about the unified scoring system location for new contributors.
recommendations.rationale: Centralized documentation of where unification lives aids future maintainers.

quality_gates.evidence: All cross-file references point to existing files with no broken links
Tier names and ranges identical across workflow and matrix with no conflicts
New unified files exist, merged files contain expected content, deleted files removed
Legacy scale mappings present and documented for transition period

summary.conclusion: STORY-434 complexity scoring unification is complete and validated. All 65 tests pass (47 unit + 18 integration). Cross-file references are intact, content is consistent across all files, file system state matches expectations, and backward compatibility is preserved. The unified 0-60 scale with 5 tiers and 4 dimensions successfully consolidates the previous conflicting scoring systems.
